Events from the **1580s in the [Spanish Netherlands](/wiki/Spanish_Netherlands "Spanish Netherlands") and [Prince-bishopric of Liège](/wiki/Prince-bishopric_of_Li%C3%A8ge "Prince-bishopric of Liège")**.

## Incumbents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1580s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Incumbents")]

### Habsburg Netherlands

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1580s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Habsburg Netherlands")]

**Monarch** – [Philip II](/wiki/Philip_II_of_Spain "Philip II of Spain"), King of Spain and [Duke of Brabant](/wiki/Dukes_of_Brabant "Dukes of Brabant"), [of Luxembourg](/wiki/Counts,_Dukes_and_Grand_Dukes_of_Luxembourg "Counts, Dukes and Grand Dukes of Luxembourg"), etc.

**Governor General** – [Alexander Farnese](/wiki/Alexander_Farnese,_Duke_of_Parma "Alexander Farnese, Duke of Parma"), Prince (later Duke) of Parma

### Prince-Bishopric of Liège

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1580s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Prince-Bishopric of Liège")]

**Prince-Bishop** – [Gerard van Groesbeeck](/wiki/Gerard_van_Groesbeeck "Gerard van Groesbeeck") to December 1580; [Ernest of Bavaria](/wiki/Ernest_of_Bavaria "Ernest of Bavaria") from January 1581

## Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1580s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Events")]

1580

* 3 March – [George de Lalaing, Count of Rennenberg](/wiki/George_de_Lalaing,_Count_of_Rennenberg "George de Lalaing, Count of Rennenberg"), reconciled to [Philip II of Spain](/wiki/Philip_II_of_Spain "Philip II of Spain")
* 9 April – [English Fury at Mechelen](/wiki/English_Fury_at_Mechelen "English Fury at Mechelen")
* 7 May – Public disputation on the Eucharist in Brussels
* 9 June – [Taking of Diest](/wiki/Taking_of_Diest_(1580) "Taking of Diest (1580)") by rebel forces
* 9 August – Unsuccessful rebel assault on [Enghien](/wiki/Enghien "Enghien")

1581

* 30 January – [Ernest of Bavaria](/wiki/Ernest_of_Bavaria "Ernest of Bavaria") elected as [Prince-bishop of Liège](/wiki/Prince-bishop_of_Li%C3%A8ge "Prince-bishop of Liège")
* 11 February – Ernest of Bavaria elected [Prince-abbot of Stavelot](/wiki/Principality_of_Stavelot-Malmedy "Principality of Stavelot-Malmedy")
* 15 June – [joyous entry](/wiki/Joyous_entry "Joyous entry") of Ernest of Bavaria in Liège
* 1 July – Catholic worship prohibited in Antwerp
* 26 July – [Union of Utrecht](/wiki/Union_of_Utrecht "Union of Utrecht") signs [Act of Abjuration](/wiki/Act_of_Abjuration "Act of Abjuration"), repudiating [Philip II of Spain](/wiki/Philip_II_of_Spain "Philip II of Spain") as sovereign of the Netherlands
* [Sack of Breda](/wiki/Capture_of_Breda_(1581) "Capture of Breda (1581)") by forces led by [Claude de Berlaymont](/wiki/Claude_de_Berlaymont "Claude de Berlaymont")
* 30 November – Alexander Farnese takes [Tournai](/wiki/Tournai "Tournai")

1582

* 19 February – festive reception of [Francis, Duke of Anjou](/wiki/Francis,_Duke_of_Anjou "Francis, Duke of Anjou") in Antwerp
* 30 April – Jesuit school opens in Liège
* 21 December – [Gregorian calendar](/wiki/Gregorian_calendar "Gregorian calendar") adopted in the Southern Netherlands

1583

* 17 January – [French Fury](/wiki/French_Fury "French Fury") at Antwerp: failed coup by [Francis, Duke of Anjou](/wiki/Francis,_Duke_of_Anjou "Francis, Duke of Anjou")
* 16 February – [Simon Stevin](/wiki/Simon_Stevin "Simon Stevin") matriculates at [Leiden University](/wiki/Leiden_University "Leiden University")
* 23 May – Ernest of Bavaria, Prince-Bishop of Liège, elected Archbishop-Elector of Cologne
* 17 June – [Battle of Steenbergen (1583)](/wiki/Battle_of_Steenbergen_(1583) "Battle of Steenbergen (1583)")
* 30 October – [Joannes Hauchin](/wiki/Joannes_Hauchin "Joannes Hauchin") consecrated as [Archbishop of Mechelen](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Mechelen "Archbishop of Mechelen")
* 2 November – [Gregorian calendar](/wiki/Gregorian_calendar "Gregorian calendar") adopted in Prince-Bishopric of Liège
* 3 November – [Taking of Aalst](/w/index.php?title=Taking_of_Aalst&action=edit&redlink=1 "Taking of Aalst (page does not exist)")
* 30 November – [Philips of Marnix, Lord of Saint-Aldegonde](/wiki/Philips_of_Marnix,_Lord_of_Saint-Aldegonde "Philips of Marnix, Lord of Saint-Aldegonde"), appointed mayor of Antwerp

1584

* 7 April – End of the [Siege of Ypres (1584)](/w/index.php?title=Siege_of_Ypres_(1584)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Siege of Ypres (1584) (page does not exist)")
* 28 May – Bruges surrendered to Alexander Farnese
* 17 August – Taking of Dendermonde
* 17 September – Surrender of Ghent

1585

* 10 March – Surrender of Brussels
* 10 August – [Treaty of Nonsuch](/wiki/Treaty_of_Nonsuch "Treaty of Nonsuch"): Elizabeth I publicly pledges support to Dutch Revolt
* 17 August – [Fall of Antwerp](/wiki/Fall_of_Antwerp "Fall of Antwerp")
* 3 November – Relics of [St Rumbold](/wiki/Rumbold_of_Mechelen "Rumbold of Mechelen") restored to [St Rumbold's Cathedral](/wiki/St_Rumbold%27s_Cathedral "St Rumbold's Cathedral")

1586

* 18 September – Alexander Farnese becomes Duke of Parma

1587

* 15 May – *Edict, ordonnance et instruction sur l'exercice et l'administration de la jurisdiction et justice militaire*

1588

* 29 May – [Jean Vendeville](/wiki/Jean_Vendeville "Jean Vendeville") consecrated [bishop of Tournai](/wiki/Bishop_of_Tournai "Bishop of Tournai")
  + 22 July – [William Damasus Lindanus](/wiki/William_Damasus_Lindanus "William Damasus Lindanus") installed as [bishop of Ghent](/wiki/Bishop_of_Ghent "Bishop of Ghent")
  + 28 July – [Battle of Gravelines](/wiki/Spanish_Armada#Battle_of_Gravelines "Spanish Armada")

1589

* Ecclesiastical censorship of the press and the theatre established by Ernest of Bavaria, Prince-Bishop of Liège, *Lettres patentes pour la conservation et maintien de la foy et religion chrestienne catholique apostolique romaine es païz de l'evesché et principauté de Liége* (printed in Liège by Gautier Morberius)

## Publications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1580s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Publications")]

1580

* *Le Renart decouvert* (Mons, [Rutger Velpius](/wiki/Rutger_Velpius "Rutger Velpius")) – a satire of [William of Orange](/wiki/William_the_Silent "William the Silent") written anonymously by [Jean Richardot](/wiki/Jean_Richardot "Jean Richardot")

1581

* Nicasius Van der Schuere, *Een cleyne of corte institutie dat is onderwysinghe der christelijcker religie ghestelt in locos communes* (Ghent, [Gauthier Manilius](/wiki/Gauthier_Manilius "Gauthier Manilius")) – a Dutch abridgement of [Calvin's *Institutes*](/wiki/Institutes_of_the_Christian_Religion "Institutes of the Christian Religion") ([available on Google Books](https://books.google.com/books?id=07ETAAAAQAAJ))
* Jean Michel, *L'anatomie du corps politique comparé au corps humain*, translated by Paul du Mont (Douai, [Jean Bogard](/wiki/Jean_Bogard "Jean Bogard")), [available on Google Books](https://books.google.com/books?id=XGVEAAAAcAAJ)

1583

* [Justus Lipsius](/wiki/Justus_Lipsius "Justus Lipsius"), *[De Constantia](/wiki/De_Constantia "De Constantia")* (Antwerp, [Plantin Press](/wiki/Plantin_Press "Plantin Press"))
* *Statuta synodi dioecesanae audomarensis, anno M D LXXXIII* (Douai, Jean Bogard) – the statutes of the diocesan synod called by [Jean Six](/wiki/Jean_Six "Jean Six") to introduce Tridentine reform in the [diocese of Saint-Omer](/wiki/Ancient_Diocese_of_Saint-Omer "Ancient Diocese of Saint-Omer")

1585

* *Articulen ende conditien vanden tractate aengegaen ende ghesloten tusschen die Prince van Parma ende de stadt van Bruessele* (Brussels, [Jan Mommaert](/wiki/Jan_Mommaert "Jan Mommaert")) – the terms of the surrender of Brussels

1587

* [Sancho de Londoño](/wiki/Sancho_de_Londo%C3%B1o "Sancho de Londoño"), *Discurso sobre la forma de reducir la disciplina militar a mejor y antiguo estado* (Brussels, Rutger Velpius)
* [William Allen's](/wiki/William_Allen_(cardinal) "William Allen (cardinal)"), *The copie of a letter concerning the yeelding up of Daventrie unto his catholike majestie, by sir William Stanley knight* (Antwerp, [Joachim Trognaesius](/wiki/Joachim_Trognaesius "Joachim Trognaesius")) – a justification of [Sir William Stanley's](/wiki/William_Stanley_(Elizabethan) "William Stanley (Elizabethan)") surrender of [Deventer](/wiki/Deventer "Deventer")
* [Floris Van der Haer](/wiki/Floris_Van_der_Haer "Floris Van der Haer"), *De initiis tumultuum Belgicorum* (Douai, [Jean Bogard](/wiki/Jean_Bogard "Jean Bogard")), the first substantial history of the origins of the [Dutch Revolt](/wiki/Dutch_Revolt "Dutch Revolt") ([available on Google Books](https://books.google.com/books?id=d-BfAAAAcAAJ))

1588

* [Jan Franco](/wiki/Jan_Franco "Jan Franco"), *Almanach oft journael voor't schrickel- jaer ons Heeren M.D.LXXXVIII* (Antwerp, Joachim Trognaesius) – a calendar for the year 1588
* *Statuta synodalia dioecesis Atrebatensis cum praedecessorum statutis adjectis* (Arras, printed by Joachim Trognaesius for Claude de Buyens) – statutes of the diocesan synod of the diocese of Arras
* [William Allen](/wiki/William_Allen_(cardinal) "William Allen (cardinal)"), *An admonition to the nobility and people of England and Ireland*, intended to be distributed in the event of a successful Spanish landing in England, printed anonymously by [Arnout Coninx](/wiki/Arnout_Coninx "Arnout Coninx") but never published

1589

* [Francisco de Valdés](/w/index.php?title=Francisco_de_Vald%C3%A9s&action=edit&redlink=1 "Francisco de Valdés (page does not exist)"), *Espeio, y deceplina militar* (Brussels, Rutger Velpius)
* I. B., *The copy of a letter lately written by a Spanishe gentleman, to his friend in England in refutation of sundry calumnies, there falsly bruited, and spred emonge the people* (Antwerp, Joachim Trognaesius) – counter-propaganda regarding the [Spanish Armada](/wiki/Spanish_Armada "Spanish Armada")
* *Cort verhael vanden aenslach die d'Engelsche hebben aenhevanghen in Spaengien ende Portugael* (Antwerp, Joachim Trognaesius) – an account of the [English Armada](/wiki/English_Armada "English Armada")'s activities on the coasts of Spain and Portugal

## Births

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1580s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Births")]

1580

* 12 January - [Jan Baptist van Helmont](/wiki/Jan_Baptist_van_Helmont "Jan Baptist van Helmont") (died 1644), alchemist
* 6 June - [Godefroy Wendelin](/wiki/Godefroy_Wendelin "Godefroy Wendelin") (died 1667), astronomer
* 9 June - [Daniel Heinsius](/wiki/Daniel_Heinsius "Daniel Heinsius") (died 1655), Dutch poet and humanist

1581

* *date unknown*
  + [Frans Francken the Younger](/wiki/Frans_Francken_the_Younger "Frans Francken the Younger") (died 1642), painter

1582

* 11 April - [Justus de Harduwijn](/wiki/Justus_de_Harduwijn "Justus de Harduwijn") (died 1636), poet
* 24 September (baptism) - [Deodat del Monte](/wiki/Deodat_del_Monte "Deodat del Monte") (died 1644), painter, engineer and art dealer
* *date unknown*
  + [Wouter Abts](/wiki/Wouter_Abts "Wouter Abts") (died 1642/3), painter
  + [Jacob van Hulsdonck](/wiki/Jacob_van_Hulsdonck "Jacob van Hulsdonck") (died 1647), painter
  + [David Teniers the Elder](/wiki/David_Teniers_the_Elder "David Teniers the Elder") (died 1649), painter

1583

* 10 April - [Nicolaus Vernulaeus](/wiki/Nicolaus_Vernulaeus "Nicolaus Vernulaeus") (died 1649), [Neo-Latin](/wiki/Neo-Latin "Neo-Latin") playwright
* 11/21 August - [Samuel Blommaert](/wiki/Samuel_Blommaert "Samuel Blommaert") (died 1651), director of the [Dutch West India Company](/wiki/Dutch_West_India_Company "Dutch West India Company")
* *date unknown*
  + [Hendrik van der Borcht the Elder](/wiki/Hendrik_van_der_Borcht_the_Elder "Hendrik van der Borcht the Elder") (died 1651), engraver and painter
  + [Henricus Calenus](/wiki/Henricus_Calenus "Henricus Calenus") (died 1653), [Jansenist](/wiki/Jansenist "Jansenist") clergyman

1584

* 12 February - [Caspar Barlaeus](/wiki/Caspar_Barlaeus "Caspar Barlaeus") (died 1648), Dutch humanist
* 18 November - [Gaspar de Crayer](/wiki/Gaspar_de_Crayer "Gaspar de Crayer") (died 1669), painter
* *date unknown*
  + [Frederick de Marselaer](/wiki/Frederick_de_Marselaer "Frederick de Marselaer") (died 1670), mayor of Brussels and author of a treatise on diplomacy
  + [Willem van Nieulandt II](/wiki/Willem_van_Nieulandt_II "Willem van Nieulandt II") (died 1635), painter and playwright
  + [Cornelis de Vos](/wiki/Cornelis_de_Vos "Cornelis de Vos") (died 1651), painter

1585

* 25 February - [Pieter van den Broecke](/wiki/Pieter_van_den_Broecke "Pieter van den Broecke") (died 1640), merchant of the [Dutch East India Company](/wiki/Dutch_East_India_Company "Dutch East India Company")
* 28 April - [Philippe d'Outreman](/wiki/Philippe_d%27Outreman "Philippe d'Outreman") (died 1652), Jesuit author
* 28 October - [Cornelius Jansen](/wiki/Cornelius_Jansen "Cornelius Jansen") (died 1638), theologian and bishop of Ypres
* 31 December - [Gonzalo de Córdoba](/wiki/Gonzalo_Fern%C3%A1ndez_de_C%C3%B3rdoba_(1585%E2%80%931645) "Gonzalo Fernández de Córdoba (1585–1645)") (died 1645), commander in the [Army of Flanders](/wiki/Army_of_Flanders "Army of Flanders")
* *date unknown*
  + [Lorenz van Steenwinckel](/wiki/Lorenz_van_Steenwinckel "Lorenz van Steenwinckel") (died 1619), architect

1586

* 15 September - [Antonius Sanderus](/wiki/Antonius_Sanderus "Antonius Sanderus") (died 1664), ecclesiastical historian
* *date unknown*
  + [Schelte a Bolswert](/wiki/Schelte_a_Bolswert "Schelte a Bolswert") (died 1659), engraver
  + [Gillis II Coignet](/wiki/Gillis_II_Coignet "Gillis II Coignet") (died 1641), painter
  + [Gijsbrecht Leytens](/wiki/Gijsbrecht_Leytens "Gijsbrecht Leytens") (died 1656), painter
  + [Francisco de Moncada, 3rd Marquis of Aitona](/wiki/Francisco_de_Moncada,_3rd_Marquis_of_Aitona "Francisco de Moncada, 3rd Marquis of Aitona") (died 1635), acting Governor General of the Spanish Netherlands, 1633-1634
  + [David Rijckaert II](/wiki/David_Rijckaert_II "David Rijckaert II") (died 1642), painter and art dealer

1587

* 17 January (baptism) - [Alexander Adriaenssen](/wiki/Alexander_Adriaenssen "Alexander Adriaenssen") (died 1661), painter
* 20 February - [Emanuel Sueyro](/wiki/Emanuel_Sueyro "Emanuel Sueyro") (died 1629), spymaster and historian
* 23 April - [Gaspard Nemius](/wiki/Gaspard_Nemius "Gaspard Nemius") (died 1667), bishop of Antwerp and archbishop of Cambrai
* 3 September - [Libert Froidmont](/wiki/Libert_Froidmont "Libert Froidmont") (died 1653), theologian and scientist
* 18 October - [Philippe-Charles, 3rd Count of Arenberg](/wiki/Philippe-Charles,_3rd_Count_of_Arenberg "Philippe-Charles, 3rd Count of Arenberg") (died 1640), statesman
* 17 November - [Louis De Geer](/wiki/Louis_De_Geer_(1587%E2%80%931652) "Louis De Geer (1587–1652)") (died 1652), arms manufacturer
* 8 December (baptism) - [Martin Ryckaert](/wiki/Martin_Ryckaert "Martin Ryckaert") (died 1631), painter
* *date unknown*
  + [Maria Faydherbe](/wiki/Maria_Faydherbe "Maria Faydherbe") (died 1643), sculptor
  + [Antonius de Liedekerke](/wiki/Antonius_de_Liedekerke "Antonius de Liedekerke") (died 1661), Dutch sea captain and diplomat
  + [Adriaen van Nieulandt](/wiki/Adriaen_van_Nieulandt "Adriaen van Nieulandt") (died 1658), Dutch painter and engraver
  + [Margriet van Noort](/wiki/Margaret_of_the_Mother_of_God "Margaret of the Mother of God") (died 1646), Carmelite mystic

1588

* 12 March - [Herman de Neyt](/wiki/Herman_de_Neyt "Herman de Neyt") (died 1642), painter and art dealer
* 9 May - [Herman Hugo](/wiki/Herman_Hugo "Herman Hugo") (died 1629), Jesuit author
* *date unknown*
  + [Jean de Beck](/wiki/Jean_de_Beck "Jean de Beck") (died 1648), governor of Luxembourg
  + [Jean-Jacques Chifflet](/wiki/Jean-Jacques_Chifflet "Jean-Jacques Chifflet") (died 1660), physician to the Brussels court, antiquary
  + [Benedictus van Haeften](/wiki/Benedictus_van_Haeften "Benedictus van Haeften") (died 1648), Benedictine author
  + [Johannes van Mildert](/wiki/Johannes_van_Mildert "Johannes van Mildert") (died 1638), sculptor

1589

* 6 April (baptism) - [Jan Tilens](/wiki/Jan_Tilens "Jan Tilens") (died 1630), painter
* 2 July (baptism) - [Guilielmus Messaus](/wiki/Guilielmus_Messaus "Guilielmus Messaus") (died 1640), composer
* 15 July (baptism) - [Cornelis Bol](/wiki/Cornelis_Bol "Cornelis Bol") (died 1666), painter and etcher
* 1 August - [Alexandrine von Taxis](/wiki/Alexandrine_von_Taxis "Alexandrine von Taxis") (died 1666), postmistress general
* *date unknown*
  + [Abraham Govaerts](/wiki/Abraham_Govaerts "Abraham Govaerts") (died 1626), painter

Year uncertain

* [Boetius à Bolswert](/wiki/Boetius_%C3%A0_Bolswert "Boetius à Bolswert") (died 1633), engraver

## Deaths

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1580s_in_the_Southern_Netherlands&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Deaths")]

1580

* 1 August - [Everard Mercurian](/wiki/Everard_Mercurian "Everard Mercurian") (born 1514)
* 15 September - [Geert van Turnhout](/wiki/Geert_van_Turnhout "Geert van Turnhout") (born about 1530), composer
* 26 October - [Anna of Austria](/wiki/Anna_of_Austria,_Queen_of_Spain "Anna of Austria, Queen of Spain") (born 1549), Queen of Spain, Duchess of Brabant, etc.
* 23 December - [Gerard van Groesbeeck](/wiki/Gerard_van_Groesbeeck "Gerard van Groesbeeck"), Prince-Bishop of Liège

1581

* 19 January - [Gillis Hooftman](/wiki/Gillis_Hooftman "Gillis Hooftman") (born 1521), merchant and shipbuilder
* 22 January - [Joos de Damhouder](/wiki/Joos_de_Damhouder "Joos de Damhouder") (born 1507), legal scholar
* 7 May - [Alexander Utendal](/wiki/Alexander_Utendal "Alexander Utendal") (born 1543/45), composer
* 13 July - [Jean Scheyfve](/wiki/Jean_Scheyfve "Jean Scheyfve") (born about 1515), former Chancellor of Brabant
* 23 July - [George de Lalaing](/wiki/George_de_Lalaing "George de Lalaing") (born about 1550), count of Rennenberg
* 19 September - [Frans Pourbus the Elder](/wiki/Frans_Pourbus_the_Elder "Frans Pourbus the Elder") (born 1545), painter
* *date unknown*
  + [Petrus Divaeus](/wiki/Petrus_Divaeus "Petrus Divaeus") (born 1535), historian
  + [Willem de Pannemaker](/wiki/Willem_de_Pannemaker "Willem de Pannemaker") (born 1512/14), tapestry designer.
  + [Nicholas Sanders](/wiki/Nicholas_Sanders "Nicholas Sanders") (born about 1530), former professor at Louvain, missing presumed dead in Ireland

1582

* 24 May - [Philip de Lalaing](/wiki/Philip_de_Lalaing_(1537%E2%80%9382) "Philip de Lalaing (1537–82)") (born 1537), governor of Hainaut
* 11 December - [Duke of Alva](/wiki/Fernando_%C3%81lvarez_de_Toledo,_3rd_Duke_of_Alba "Fernando Álvarez de Toledo, 3rd Duke of Alba") (born 1507), former Governor of the Netherlands (1567–1573)
* *date unknown*
  + [Arnoldus Arlenius](/wiki/Arnoldus_Arlenius "Arnoldus Arlenius") (born about 1510), humanist
  + [Hans Hendrik van Paesschen](/wiki/Hans_Hendrik_van_Paesschen "Hans Hendrik van Paesschen") (born about 1510), architect

1583



1584

* 10 July - [William of Orange](/wiki/William_the_Silent "William the Silent") (born 1533) assassinated in Delft

1585

* 10 March - [Rembert Dodoens](/wiki/Rembert_Dodoens "Rembert Dodoens") (born 1517), botanist

1586

* 18 January - [Margaret of Parma](/wiki/Margaret_of_Parma "Margaret of Parma") (born 1522), former Governor of the Netherlands (1559–1567 and 1578–1582)
* 21 September - [Antoine Perrenot de Granvelle](/wiki/Antoine_Perrenot_de_Granvelle "Antoine Perrenot de Granvelle") (born 1517), former Archbishop of Mechelen (1561–1582)
* 11 October - [Jean Six](/wiki/Jean_Six "Jean Six") (born 1533), bishop of [Saint-Omer](/wiki/Saint-Omer "Saint-Omer")

1587

* 14 July - [Claude de Berlaymont](/wiki/Claude_de_Berlaymont "Claude de Berlaymont") (born ca. 1550), nobleman and commander
* 19 September - [Jacobus Pamelius](/wiki/Jacobus_Pamelius "Jacobus Pamelius") (born 1536), theologian

1588

* 17 March - [Petrus Dathenus](/wiki/Petrus_Dathenus "Petrus Dathenus") (born about 1531), translator of the [Heidelberg Catechism](/wiki/Heidelberg_Catechism "Heidelberg Catechism") and of metrical psalms into Dutch
* 2 November - [William Damasus Lindanus](/wiki/William_Damasus_Lindanus "William Damasus Lindanus"), [Bishop of Ghent](/wiki/Bishop_of_Ghent "Bishop of Ghent")

1589

* 5 January - [Joannes Hauchin](/wiki/Joannes_Hauchin "Joannes Hauchin") (born 1527), second [Archbishop of Mechelen](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Mechelen "Archbishop of Mechelen")
* 22 March - [Lodovico Guicciardini](/wiki/Lodovico_Guicciardini "Lodovico Guicciardini") (born 1521), Florentine merchant in Antwerp
* 1 July - [Christophe Plantin](/wiki/Christophe_Plantin "Christophe Plantin") (born about 1520), printer and bookseller in Antwerp
